title: IPL 2026: 5 players who could replace injured Ayush Mhatre in CSK
content: Chennai Super Kings face a setback as Ayush Mhatre is out of IPL 2026 due to a hamstring injury. This adds to their existing injury concerns. The team is now looking for replacements to maintain their momentum. Several players like Deepak Hooda, Swastik Chikara, Atharva Taide, Manan Vohra, and Yash Dhull are being considered to fill the void.

title: Who is Johny Srouji, Apple’s new head of hardware engineering division as John Ternus named new CEO
content: Apple has appointed John Ternus as its new CEO, effective September 1, succeeding Tim Cook. In a significant move, Johny Srouji has been promoted to Chief Hardware Officer, expanding his leadership to encompass Hardware Engineering and Hardware Technologies. Srouji's expertise in custom chip development has been pivotal to Apple's product innovation.

title: ‘Zero impact’: Ex-India captain roasts Parag, Rahane in worst batter debate
content: Former captain Krishnamachari Srikkanth has sharply criticized Riyan Parag's batting form in the IPL, questioning his impact and comparing him unfavorably to Ajinkya Rahane. Srikkanth also believes Mumbai Indians are poised for a playoff surge, while dismissing Kolkata Knight Riders and Lucknow Super Giants' chances. He favors Sunrisers Hyderabad over Delhi Capitals, citing a batting concern.

title: Fortuner at gate, gunshots at 2am: How a parking feud turned fatal in Delhi's Preet Vihar
content: A parking dispute over a Fortuner tragically escalated into murder in East Delhi's Preet Vihar early Monday. Pankaj Nayyar, 34, was shot dead outside his family home by a tenant neighbour, Gaurav Sharma, after an argument over vehicle placement. Sharma, along with two accomplices, allegedly attacked Pankaj and his brother before fleeing.

title: IPL 2026: KL Rahul closing in on MS Dhoni’s record, set to overtake CSK legend
content: KL Rahul is on the cusp of a significant IPL milestone, needing just 50 runs against Sunrisers Hyderabad to surpass MS Dhoni as the sixth-highest run-scorer. Rahul's aggressive batting this season, marked by a strike rate near 170, has seen him amass 168 runs so far. He has already outscored Dhoni in keeper-specific 50+ scores.

title: Amazon files lawsuit after losing millions in fake laptop and GPU refunds
content: A sophisticated refund scam has cost Amazon $4 million in stolen goods like graphics cards and laptops. A group named RBK allegedly exploited customer support by claiming packages never arrived, even using fake police reports. Amazon uncovered the scheme through an undercover investigator, leading to a lawsuit against named individuals and unknown accomplices.

title: 'Daddy me dum ho toh rok lena': BJP MLA’s viral warning to cop after son rams 5 with Thar
content: A political storm brews in Shivpuri after a video surfaced showing BJP MLA Pritam Lodhi confronting police.  Lodhi allegedly warned Karera SDOP Ayush Jakhad regarding action taken against his son.  The incident follows an accident where an SUV, reportedly driven by the MLA's son, injured five people. Police maintain due procedure was followed in the investigation.

title: 'Bhooth Bangla' BO day 5: Akshay Kumar starrer chases Rs 100cr domestic milestone
content: 'Bhooth Bangla' Day 5 box office tracking shows the Akshay Kumar and Priyadarshan film crossing the Rs. 100 crore mark globally. Despite a 70% drop on its first Monday, the horror-comedy remains a topic of discussion for its nostalgic cast including Paresh Rawal and Rajpal Yadav. As of April 21, 2026, the domestic net collection stands at Rs. 65.46 crore.

title: 'Never been more ...':Tim Cook in memo to employees on his exit as Apple CEO
content: Apple CEO Tim Cook announced his transition to executive chairman later this year, after a 15-year tenure. He expressed confidence in Apple's future and its core values, praising the team's dedication. John Ternus, SVP of hardware engineering, will succeed Cook as CEO, with Cook pledging to support the smooth transition.

title: Ayush Mhatre injury bombshell: CSK youngster ruled out of IPL 2026
content: Chennai Super Kings' Ayush Mhatre is out of IPL 2026 due to a left hamstring injury sustained on April 18 against Sunrisers Hyderabad. The franchise confirmed the batter requires six to twelve weeks of rehabilitation, ending his season. Mhatre continued batting after the initial injury before its severity was assessed.

title: ICSE Class 10 Result 2026: Viral “tomorrow release” claim false, CISCE expected to announce in late April
content: A viral claim suggesting that the ICSE Class 10 Result 2026 will be declared tomorrow has been denied by the Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE). An official clarified that no result date has been announced and the circulating notice is fake. However, results are likely to be released in the last week of April 2026. Students are advised to rely only on official CISCE websites for updates and avoid misinformation on social media.

title: How Sawan Barwal Broke India’s Longest-Standing Record and Still Wanted More
content: Sawan Barwal broke India’s 48-year-old marathon national record in his debut at Rotterdam, but the achievement was overshadowed by disappointment over missing his target. Barwal looks back at the race, the collapse in the closing stretch, the support system behind the run and the reason for switching from 5000m, 10000m to marathon.

title: 'Rahul Gandhi se chalne wala nahi hai': Tej Pratap Yadav backs Priyanka as INDIA bloc face
content: Tej Pratap Yadav endorsed Priyanka Gandhi to lead the Congress, deeming her capable like Indira Gandhi, while questioning Rahul Gandhi's leadership. This statement followed Rahul Gandhi's criticism of Nitish Kumar and came amid existing doubts about Rahul's ability to manage the party effectively, with allegations of centralized decision-making and sidelining senior leaders.

title: ‘Then lots of bombs start going off’: Trump on what will happen when Iran ceasefire expires
content: US President Trump has warned of a significant escalation in the Iran conflict, stating "lots of bombs start going off" if a peace deal isn't reached before the current ceasefire expires.  While a US delegation prepares for talks, Iran's participation remains uncertain, with Tehran resisting negotiations under pressure.  Washington's core demand is Iran's renunciation of nuclear weapons.

title: 'This will be my wedding gift': UP groom swaps mandap for morcha over power bill
content: A groom in Banda, facing a pre-wedding power cut, led his baraat to the district magistrate's office demanding a bill waiver.  Kuldeep cited a doubled bill after a smart meter installation, making payment difficult. Officials assured a resolution, prompting the groom to proceed with his wedding, with an inquiry ordered into the disconnection.

title: 'TMC goons switched off lights during BJP roadshow': Delhi CM Rekha Gupta
content: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta alleged TMC "goons" switched off streetlights during her Kolkata roadshow, calling it an attempt to disrupt the event and create a security risk. She stated the public responded by using mobile flashlights, signaling their readiness to support the BJP. Gupta expressed confidence in the BJP's victory in the upcoming West Bengal Assembly elections.

title: 'Narrative played on his mind’: R Ashwin explains Jasprit Bumrah’s dip in form
content: Jasprit Bumrah finally claimed his first wicket of IPL 2026, ending a six-match drought. Following Mumbai Indians' dominant win over Gujarat Titans, Ravichandran Ashwin believes this breakthrough will allow Bumrah to perform at his peak.

title: ‘Kejriwal is a bully’: Bansuri Swaraj after Delhi high court judge refuses to recuse
content: Delhi High Court dismissed Arvind Kejriwal's plea to recuse Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma from the excise policy case, deeming it based on conjecture. BJP MP Bansuri Swaraj accused Kejriwal of pressuring the judiciary. Justice Sharma clarified her participation in professional events and family government counsel roles did not indicate bias, stating the plea put the judiciary on trial.

title: WATCH: ‘Celebrating’ runner loses marathon in final seconds as rival steals win
content: A dramatic finish at the Delaware Marathon saw Joshua Jackson snatch victory from Carson Mello in the final metres. Mello, celebrating prematurely, was overtaken by a determined Jackson in a stunning upset. The viral footage serves as a potent reminder that a race is never truly won until the finish line is crossed, highlighting the importance of sustained focus.

title: AI scams costing millions: Experts warn and share safety tips
content: Fraudsters are using advanced AI to create convincing fake videos and voices. They impersonate executives, celebrities and even loved ones to trick people into sending money.  Cybersecurity experts warn that these deepfake scams are becoming more common and sophisticated. Staying vigilant and verifying unexpected requests through separate channels is crucial for protection against these evolving threats.

title: ‘Horrible day for Gujarat Titans’: Hayden blasts ‘unacceptable’ collapse in MI hammering
content: Gujarat Titans' batting coach Matthew Hayden slammed his team's 99-run defeat to Mumbai Indians, calling the batting 'unacceptable' and a 'horrible day'. He highlighted a powerplay collapse that exposed the middle order prematurely and criticised the bowling for conceding too many runs late. Hayden stressed the need for better mindset and execution from his star-studded lineup.

title: What happens if you eat avocado daily for 14 days? Doctor explains
content: A 14-day avocado challenge is gaining traction, with experts suggesting daily consumption can lead to significant physiological changes. This trend is supported by recent research indicating improvements in cardiovascular health, gut microbiome diversity and blood pressure regulation within this short timeframe. The benefits stem from avocados' rich monounsaturated fats, fiber and potassium content.

title: All about Julie Lee Choi: The stalker who claimed to be Tim Cook's wife and terrorised him
content: Tim Cook steps down as Apple CEO, transitioning to executive chairman, with John Ternus taking the helm. Cook's tenure was marked by significant achievements and a bizarre 2020 stalking incident involving a woman who falsely claimed to be his wife and mother of his children. Apple secured a restraining order against the individual, highlighting the challenges faced by public figures.

title: Watch: BJP leader Nazia enters Lenskart store, applies tilak on staff amid dress code row
content: A political leader has entered a dispute over Lenskart's employee dress code. The leader defended Hindu religious symbols after allegations of restrictions. Lenskart has since apologized and pledged to revise its guidelines. The company stated it welcomes all symbols of faith and culture. This incident has broadened the debate on workplace expression and religious identity.

title: TMC MP Yusuf Pathan’s kin beat Mumbai man, his family as car splashes water
content: Yusuf Pathan's father-in-law and two others were arrested for allegedly assaulting a man and his family in Byculla. The incident began when a car splashed water on a relative, leading to an argument and subsequent physical assault with bamboo sticks and a baseball bat. The defence claims both parties were involved in the altercation.

title: NCP (SP) president Sharad Pawar admitted to Mumbai hospital
content: Veteran politician Sharad Pawar, president of the Nationalist Congress Party (SP), has been admitted to Mumbai's Breach Candy Hospital for a routine health check-up. The 85-year-old leader is undergoing tests and follow-up procedures, with sources confirming there is no serious concern. This comes after Pawar recently took his Rajya Sabha oath from a wheelchair.

title: Delhi HC holds Hockey India, secy general Bhola Nath Singh guilty of contempt
content: The Delhi High Court has found Hockey India and its secretary general guilty of contempt for wilfully disobeying a prior order. Officials were directed to allow Syeed Asima Ali access to executive board meetings but failed to provide necessary links for multiple sessions.

title: Prince Yadav: From escaping dad’s beatings to beating batters with pace in IPL 2026
content: Prince Yadav, an LSG pacer, honed his skills playing tennis-ball cricket in his village. He still plays the game, crediting it for his arm speed and yorkers. Despite a past age-fudging ban, he is now a rising uncapped Indian fast bowler. He learns from seasoned players like Mohammed Shami and Zaheer Khan.

title: Announced, relaunched, delayed, burned: Pachpadra refinery’s troubled timeline
content: Rajasthan's ambitious greenfield refinery project, plagued by political interference and cost escalations, faces further delay after a fire incident in its main Crude Distillation Unit. The Rs 79,459 crore project, with 92% work completed, was set for inauguration, but the blaze has cast doubt on its commissioning timeline.

title: Japan training drill turns fatal: 3 soldiers killed in tank misfire
content: Three Japanese soldiers were killed and one injured when their tank's ammunition misfired during a training exercise in Oita. Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi described the incident as "truly regrettable" and offered condolences as the cause is under investigation.

title: Pakistan pauses $1.5 billion Sudan arms deal after Saudi objection: Report
content: Pakistan has halted a $1.5 billion weapons and fighter jet deal with Sudan following Saudi Arabia's request to terminate the arrangement and withdraw financing. This development occurs amidst Sudan's ongoing conflict and intensifies regional rivalries, with Saudi Arabia previously brokering the deal.

title: US limits intelligence sharing with Korea after remarks on North Korea nuclear site: Report
content: US has partially restricted satellite intelligence sharing with South Korea after Seoul's unification minister revealed details about a suspected North Korean uranium enrichment site. While the minister claims the information was public, Washington views it as an unauthorized disclosure.  Despite this, officials state military preparedness and broader intelligence cooperation remain unaffected.

title: US starts refund process for Trump tariffs: Can Indian exporters claim?
content: Exporters and end consumers are not permitted to file claims directly, although some companies, such as FedEx, may opt to pass on the refunded amounts at their discretion.

title: Allahabad high court judge recuses himself from Rahul Gandhi's dual citizenship case
content: Justice Subhash Vidyarthi recused himself from hearing Rahul Gandhi's dual citizenship case after being peeved by petitioner's social media posts. The judge admitted he should have issued notice to Gandhi before dictating an FIR order, and chastised lawyers for not providing correct legal positions on hearing the accused.

title: West Bengal polls: 9 arrested after BJP-TMC clash outside Kolkata police station
content: Political clashes erupted outside Kolkata's Ekbalpur Police Station, leading to the arrest of nine individuals, including BJP and TMC members. The violence, fueled by counter-sloganeering during election campaigning, prompted police intervention and the registration of three FIRs. This incident heightens tensions as West Bengal gears up for crucial Assembly elections.

title: Tilak Varma outguns Gujarat Titans as record-breaking blitz rewrites IPL history
content: Tilak Varma delivered a historic IPL century, smashing 101 off 45 balls to lead Mumbai Indians to a dominant 99-run victory.  His explosive innings, featuring a remarkable turnaround in scoring rate, saw him outscore the entire Gujarat Titans lineup. Varma's maiden century also marked a personal milestone, earning him his first Player-of-the-Match award.

title: Pumpkin seed oil for hair: How to make pumpkin seed oil for super hair growth in summer
content: Summer's toll on hair can be mitigated with a simple, natural solution: pumpkin seed oil. Packed with zinc and healthy fats, this oil nourishes the scalp, reduces hair fall, and supports healthier growth over time.

title: Watch: Yogi attributes Subhas Chandra Bose's ‘give me blood' to Swami Vivekananda
content: Uttar Pradesh CM Yogi Adityanath's misattribution of Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose's famous "give me blood, I will give you freedom" slogan to Swami Vivekananda has sparked widespread criticism. Trinamool and Samajwadi Party leaders condemned the remark, highlighting the BJP's alleged ignorance of Bengal's history and the freedom struggle. The slogan was originally delivered by Netaji to INA soldiers in Burma.

title: T.N. Assembly polls: Musiri residents flag water, roads and livelihood issues
content: Musiri, an agriculturally fertile constituency on the banks of the Cauvery in Tiruchi district, is known for its rich history, vibrant farming economy and traditional crafts. As the State Assembly elections approach, residents point to challenges such as groundwater depletion, irrigation planning, road connectivity, water supply and the future of cottage industries, even as agriculture and banana cultivation continue to sustain livelihoods.

title: Several injured in blast at fireworks unit in Kerala’s Thrissur ahead of Pooram
content: At least eight workers critically injured in explosion at facility preparing materials for Thiruvambady wing for Thrissur Pooram. Unconfirmed reports suggest around 40 people may have been inside the unit

title: India is violating its obligations under international law: Francesca Albanese
content: The UN Special Rapporteur speaks on her latest report on the situation of human rights in the occupied Palestinian territories, flags legal and moral concerns for countries backing Israel

title: Both are actors who took the political route, but MGR-Vijay is a facile comparison
content: While MGR spent a long time in politics before he launched AIADMK, Vijay had no boots on the ground, until he formed the TVK, the political party

title: Bengaluru’s scorching summer heat in the last decade
content: The normal maximum temperature for Bengaluru during the months of March, April, and May are 33.4°C, 34.1°C, and 33.1°C, respectively. However, in the last decade, on several occasions, above normal temperature has been recorded during these three months

title: Only marks for SSLC third language, no grades this year: Karnataka High Court
content: Any proposal for awarding grades for third language could be considered only from the next academic year as per law, states HC

title: Mamata's associate Humayun Kabir wants to build Babri Masjid in Bengal; won't allow it: Amit Shah
content: The senior BJP leader also promised that a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) would be implemented in the State if the party came to power

title: Row over BJP MLA’s remarks after son’s SUV rams into five people
content: Legislator Pritam Lodhi's son Dinesh is accused of ramming his SUV, into three young men riding a motorcycle and two women walking on road under the Karera police station area of Shivpuri district on Thursday, leaving them injured.

title: Despite being in DMK alliance, DMDK works for AIADMK alliance: Edappadi Palaniswami and Anbumani
content: Mr. Palaniswami claimed the DMDK was indirectly supporting the AIADMK alliance. “Truth cannot be hidden. [DMDK leader] Premallatha Vijayakant is speaking the truth [her recent utterances on law and order], and we thank her,” he said.

title: Gujarat ATS cracks alleged ‘Ghazwa-e-Hind’ conspiracy module, two held
content: According to investigators, the suspects were allegedly working towards building a network with the intent of carrying out attacks and promoting the idea of “Ghazwa-e-Hind”, while also exploring the use of explosive materials such as RDX

title: T.N. Assembly polls: Ponneri locals flag jobs and transport issues
content: Residents of the Ponneri Assembly constituency in Tiruvallur district raise concerns over limited employment opportunities, inadequate transport facilities, and lack of visible benefits from large industries and power plants operating in the region. Locals also reiterate demands for better bus services, improved suburban rail connectivity, and consideration of Ponneri as a separate district.

title: Andhra accepts Seeta Sharma’s resignation as International Skilling Advisor
content: Ms. Sharma, appointed for two years in January 2025, resigned on March 5 citing operational and structural constraints; the government order was issued on Tuesday

title: Nithin Raj death: Action council calls Statewide hartal on April 28
content: The deceased’s family has alleged lapses in police investigation and sought a probe under High Court supervision

title: DGP to probe harassment allegations, concerns of SP regarding Mangaluru police chief: Karnataka Home Minister
content: The DCRE Inspector, identified as Srinivas, has levelled serious allegations about misuse of power by IPS officers while SP Kashinath has written to the DGP highlighting his concerns

title: Winners of The Hindu Best Places to Work Awards 2026
content: When organisations prioritise their people, results are transformative — there is stronger talent retention, higher productivity, and enhanced innovation

title: Assamese film ‘Aakuti’ heads to New York Indian Film Festival 2026
content: Snigdha P Roy on her Assamese debut, working with a first-time child actor, crafting emotion, and taking the film from short to feature

title: Watch: Arvind Kejriwal: ‘M.K. Stalin has stood for the country, for democracy’
content: Arvind Kejriwal praises the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam and M. K. Stalin for their strong support, welfare policies, and governance in Tamil Nadu, saying they have brought significant relief to the poor and middle class. He highlights Stalin’s role in defending democracy and federalism at the national level, criticizes the Bharatiya Janata Party and its alliance with All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam, and warns that a BJP-led government could reverse the state’s progress.

title: Kerala Sanskrit varsity row: HoD’s alleged warning over Governor’s event, send-off attendance sparks SFI protest
content: Audio clip, which circulated before a send-off event and inauguration of Sree Sankara Jayanti celebrations, reportedly warned students of potential faculty non-cooperation if they did not attend

title: India’s butterfly boom: How Nature walks, parks, and trails are sparking a new conservation movement
content: From Bengaluru’s urban forests to the Northeast’s biodiversity hotspots, enthusiasts and experts are turning butterfly-watching into a powerful gateway to ecology, education, and sustainable travel

title: Ban on Telangana Govt employees’ transfers relaxed temporarily; to be reimposed from June 1, 2026
content: Employees of certain Telangana government departments can be transferred between May 1 and 31. Detailed guidelines issued.

title: Pakistan extends airspace closure for Indian planes till May 24
content: According to a NOTAM (Notice to Airmen), Pakistan airspace is not available for Indian registered aircraft and aircraft operated/owned or leased by Indian airlines/ operators, including military flights

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Poll bugle to fall silent across State on April 21
content: All political functionaries and party workers who are not voters of a constituency must clear out immediately after 6 p.m. Tuesday

title: Maharashtra government employees begin indefinite strike over pension notification
content: About 1.7 million staff join strike as government invokes ‘No Work, No Pay’ rule and warns of action under Maharashtra Essential Services Maintenance Act

title: West Bengal polls - phase 1: 294 candidates face serious criminal charges, 98 booked for crimes against women
content: One in five candidates has serious criminal cases, 98 face charges of crimes against women — yet women make up a mere 11% of West Bengal’s first-phase candidates, finds ADR

title: BJP always fails to deliver on promises, TMC's welfare schemes to continue uninterrupted: Abhishek
content: Addressing a poll rally at Barjora in Bankura district, Mr. Banerjee said women “will continue to receive benefits under the scheme for life”

title: Congress MP gives privilege notice against PM in Lok Sabha for ‘casting aspersions’ on members
content: PM Modi criticised opposition parties for blocking the bill and made direct reflections on the voting pattern of Members of the Opposition and attributed motives to them, K.C. Venugopal said

title: Retired High Court Judge Amitav Kumar Gupta sworn in as Jharkhand Lokayukta
content: Governor Santosh Kumar Gangwar administered the oath of office to him during a ceremony at the Lok Bhavan in Ranch

title: Six Andhra Ministers in Singapore to study urban governance under Swarna Andhra Vision 2047
content: The delegation, on a tour from April 21 to 27, will study the city-state’s administrative systems and take part in a training programme under an agreement with the Singapore government and the World Bank

title: Fireflies can never dispel the darkness: Dravidar Kazhagam president K. Veeramani
content: They sparkle briefly and vanish. Only electricity gives sustained light. That electricity is the DMK-led alliance. Its path is permanent, according to Dravidar Kazhagam president K. Veeramani. He says actor-politician Vijay seems to have applied the ‘work from home’ philosophy to politics

title: Shankara Jayanthi marked at Srisailam temple with special rituals
content: Temple priests performed Maha Ganapati puja and special abhishekams for the deities; rituals were also held for a new Rudramurthy idol consecrated earlier in Vaisakha

title: Ayanur residents question decision to shut down well-equipped village Community Health Centre while staff quarters being built
content: Locals are questioning the rationale behind the closure when the facility already has robust infrastructure in place

title: MGNREGA implementation has weakened under TDP-led NDA government, says Jagan
content: The YSRCP chief, citing the scheme’s data, says the approved labour budget, person-days and household completions have fallen over the past two years

title: Only ‘double engine’ government can bring ‘bullet speed growth’ to Tamil Nadu: Chandrababu Naidu
content: Comparing Chennai with other cities like Bengaluru and Hyderabad, Mr. Naidu said the growth of Chennai had stagnated. According to him, the present facilities at Chennai airport are no match for those available at the Bengaluru and Hyderabad airports.

title: Civil servants must work within rule of law, says Chief Secretary Sai Prasad
content: Speaking at the 18th Civil Services Day in Amaravati, the Chief Secretary urges officers to keep upgrading their skills, and to be firm in administration but humane with people

title: Kerala polls: After Perambra row, Election Commission orders strongrooms not to be opened before counting day
content: Kerala Chief Electoral Officer Rathan U. Kelkar issues direction after reports of a strongroom storing EVMs in Perambra constituency in Kozhikode being opened on Monday, and reported plans to open another one in Nenmara constituency in Palakkad

title: Opening of ‘polling material room’ in Kozhikode kicks up a row
content: UDF candidates of six constituencies in Kozhikode say strong room was opened under the supervision of officials without prior intimation to candidates; Collector and Returning officer for Perambra say an unsealed room housing polling materials was opened in the presence of candidates’ agents to collect data for updating poll software

title: Life feels impossible without a partner, says Widow of 'ponywallah' killed in Pahalgam terror attack
content: "I was married to Adil for six years. He was the sole earning member of the family and took full responsibility for our 4-5 member household. Since his death, life has been hard," says Gulnaz Akhtar

title: Watch: T.R.B. Rajaa: ‘Tamil Nadu will never ever be under the control of Delhi’
content: DMK IT wing leader and Industries Minister T.R.B. Rajaa is dismissive about the political entry of actor C. Joseph Vijay. In an interview with The Hindu, he shared thoughts on the Assembly elections, his performance, and more.

title: Waiting for another take-off: Dealing with U.P.’s stalled airports
content: Over the past few years, several airports have been inaugurated across second- and third-tier cities of Uttar Pradesh to boost regional connectivity. However, operations at a number of these have been discontinued within weeks of launch, prompting questions over planning, viability and the gap between infrastructure expansion and sustained services

title: ‘Will get justice when country becomes terrorism-free’: family of Pahalgam attack victim recalls horrors of April 22
content: Pragati Jagdale, wife of the late Santosh Jagdale, recalled the horrors of April 22, 2025, when Pakistan-backed terrorists entered a village of Pahalgam and killed 26 individuals

title: Marginal drop in Telangana’s maximum temperatures on April 20; highest of 43.4℃ in Mancherial
content: Thunderstorms were predicted in 11 Telangana districts, according to a forecast valid till 4.00 p.m. on April 21, 2026

title: Noted writer Madhu Kishwar booked for ‘misleading’ social media post
content: Ms. Kishwar, in a social media post, said a team of Chandigarh police had visited her late Monday night to serve her notice regarding the FIR registered against her

title: Women’s quota: Congress recalls Rahul, Sonia letters to PM, says government ‘slept’ on this demand
content: The Opposition party had also demanded that the Centre immediately implement the women's quota on the existing Lok Sabha strength by bringing a Bill in Parliament's Monsoon Session or May-end

title: MSC Elsa 3 shipwreck off Kerala: Tamil Nadu seeks ₹1,041.8-crore compensation for pollution impact, Union Ministry tells NGT
content: NGT Principal Bench asks Ministry to submit details of States and Union Territories that may have suffered environmental damage owing to the shipwreck after it took suo motu cognisance of a report in The Hindu

title: Cuddalore-Puducherry administrations hold inter-State coordination meeting ahead of Tamil Nadu polls
content: The joint meeting was chaired by Cuddalore Collector Sibi Adhithya Senthil Kumar and Puducherry Collector A. Kulothungan.

title: DMK, Congress have betrayed women and the country: Chandrababu Naidu
content: Mr. Naidu said he had been fighting for higher reservation for women for the last three decades. Mr. Modi, he claimed, wanted to implement it in the 2029 elections, and so, he announced a 50% increase in seats for all States. Women are aspiring and are waiting for this for so long.

title: Andhra Pradesh to spend ₹30 crore on canal, tank revival in Srikakulam under Jaladhara
content: The work will cover silt removal, bund strengthening and tank-to-tank flow, and is expected to irrigate about 30,000 acres

title: Election Commission will leave no stone unturned for free, fair and transparent election in West Bengal: CEC
content: The election will be ‘fear free, violence free, intimidation free, inducement free, chappa free, ⁠booth-jamming free, source-jamming free’, Gyanesh Kumar says

title: McCollum plays MSG villain as Hawks tie series
content: CJ McCollum became the latest to join the list of Madison Square Garden villains as his fourth-quarter exploits helped the Hawks tie their first-round series against the Knicks with a come-from-behind 107-106 win in Game 2.

title: Gobert carries DPOY snub into Jokic matchup, win
content: On the same day he finished fourth in Defensive Player of the Year voting, Timberwolves center Rudy Gobert held three-time MVP Nikola Jokic to 1-of-8 shooting in their Game 2 matchup, helping Minnesota to a series-tying 119-114 win.

title: Martinook lifts Canes in 2OT after goal overturn
content: Jordan Martinook, who was stopped on a penalty shot in the first overtime session after a potential winning goal was overturned, beat Linus Ullmark at 13:53 of the second overtime to help the Carolina Hurricanes past the Ottawa Senators 3-2 Monday night.

title: Wemby captures DPOY as first unanimous winner
content: Victor Wembanyama, 22, is both the youngest DPOY winner in NBA history and the first to win the award unanimously.

title: Dodgers' Diaz to have elbow surgery, miss months
content: Los Angeles Dodgers closer Edwin Diaz is scheduled to undergo surgery Wednesday to remove loose bodies in his right elbow, the team announced Monday, and the expectation is that he could miss around three months, a source told ESPN's Alden Gonzalez.

title: PGA Tour boots both Hawaii events from '27 sked
content: The PGA Tour will not be in Hawaii in 2027 for the first time in 56 years as it moves toward a revamped schedule that no longer will start the season in the middle of the Pacific Ocean.

title: Counsell: MLB rule in place for Ohtani 'bizarre'
content: Chicago Cubs manager Craig Counsell thinks that a Major League Baseball rule that allows the Los Angeles Dodgers to carry 14 pitchers because of Shohei Ohtani is "bizarre," adding that the defending National League MVP is getting "special consideration."

title: 🏈 Jordan Reid's seven-round NFL mock draft
content: Jordan Reid projected every single pick for this week's NFL draft, from the Raiders' selection at No. 1 to Mr. Irrelevant.

title: Kiper's final 2026 Big Board: Ranking the top 150 NFL draft prospects, plus position reports
content: Mel Kiper Jr. updated his NFL draft rankings one last time. His Big Board stacks 150 prospects -- starting with Fernando Mendoza -- and goes deep at every position.

title: LeBron does it again, and one team has a big problem: Windhorst on the first weekend of the playoffs
content: A 25-year-old LeBron strategy for the Lakers, a new defense in Cleveland and a message from Oklahoma City: Here's what we learned as the postseason tipped off.

title: Even in retirement, Chris Paul's legacy is rooted in this season's playoffs
content: Dozens of NBA players have been mentored by the future Hall of Famer, and many of them are vying for the title.

title: WrestleMania 42 Night 2 takeaways: Will Reigns make this title run alone?
content: Roman Reigns is champion once again, but what should fans expect in the latest title reign of the OTC?

title: The Warriors begin an offseason with massive uncertainty
content: Golden State enters another offseason in search of a co-star for Stephen Curry, and the Warriors might be searching for a new coach too.
